title: oenothera pallidula
intro: Oenothera pallidula, also known as the pale evening primrose, is a perennial plant species in the evening primrose family that is native to the western United States. The medicinal properties of Oenothera pallidula lie in its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a fatty acid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as its ability to soothe skin conditions and reduce inflammation. Oenothera pallidula is often used in herbal preparations, including supplements and teas, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water.
